objective: By the end of the lesson, learners will have practised and developed the sub-skills of reading for gist and detail in the context of politeness across cultures.
materials:
- Skilful Level 2, Chapter 1, pp. 8-12
- YouTube video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mLIv1WH5u4Q

stages.stage_aim: To get learners thinking about how everyday behaviours can be seen as polite or rude, and introduce the lesson topic.
stages.procedure:
- Display the phubbing image. Ask: "What's happening in this picture? Is this polite or rude?" Elicit responses. 2 min.
- Pair discussion: "Is it rude to look at your phone when you are with other people?" Share ideas. 2 min.
- Broaden: "What other behaviours are polite in one culture but might be considered rude in another?" Draw out examples — tipping, eye contact, left hand. Bridge to lesson topic. 2 min.
- Introduce objectives: today we will study a reading about politeness and watch a video on table manners. 2 min.

stages.stage_aim: To pre-teach key topic vocabulary and activate learners' existing opinions on polite behaviours through a rating task.
stages.procedure:
- Exercise A1 (Vocabulary preview): Learners match 8 topic words (behaviour, consider, judge, manners, rude, smiling, social media, tip) to sentences. 5 min.
- Check answers with three clickthrough answer slides (3+3+2).
- Exercise B (Before you read — survey): Learners rate 4 behaviours from 1 (fine) to 10 (very rude): speaking on phone on public transport, interrupting, using left hand to greet, sending emails during a meeting. Compare ratings with a partner. 4 min.

stages.stage_aim: To develop listening for specific information through a short video on table manners across cultures.
stages.procedure:
- Transition into video: announce we will watch a clip about table manners training for international business workers.
- Play the video "Table Manners" (YouTube: mLIv1WH5u4Q).
- While you watch: Learners read 3 MCQ questions, watch video, circle answers. 3 min.
- Check answers as a class using clickthrough answer slides.
- Brief discussion: Would you take this course? Why or why not?

stages.stage_aim: To practise identifying topic sentences and matching main ideas to paragraphs in a reading text about politeness.
stages.procedure:
- Transition into reading: announce we will read a text about politeness across generations, cultures, and technology.
- Pedagogical slide: Explain topic sentences with an example from paragraph 3 of the text ("From tipping, to smiling..."). Show how the rest of the paragraph supports the topic sentence.
- Exercise C1 (Global reading — topic sentences): Learners underline the topic sentence in paragraphs 2-4 of the text. 0.5 min.
- Exercise C2 (Global reading — matching): Learners match three main ideas (A: People use different phrases, B: Technology changed norms, C: Politeness varies across cultures) to paragraphs 2-4. 5 min.
- Check C2 answers as a class using clickthrough answer slide.

stages.stage_aim: To practise scanning for specific information by completing a gap-fill summary based on the reading text.
stages.procedure:
- Transition: announce we will read more carefully to find exact words.
- Exercise D (Close reading): Learners reread the text and complete a 7-blank summary paragraph individually. Emphasise scanning — the exact words are in the text. 6 min.
- Check answers as a class using clickthrough answer slide with justifications.

stages.stage_aim: To give learners the opportunity to discuss their own views on politeness and consolidate key takeaways from the lesson.
stages.procedure:
- Transition into discussion phase.
- Exercise E (Critical thinking): In small groups, discuss: "What behaviour do you find rude?", "Would others find your behaviour rude?", "Are younger people less polite?" Refer back to the reading. 5 min.
- Return to opening question: "Is it rude to look at your phone when you are with other people? After today's lesson, has your answer changed?" Invite 2-3 learners to share. 3 min.
